title: On Building Things
excerpt: There is something quietly radical about deciding to make something from nothing. Not because the world needs more things, but because you do.
content:
There is something quietly radical about deciding to make something from nothing. Not because the world needs more things, but because you do.

Building is an act of faith. You start with an idea that exists only in your head — fragile, undefined, embarrassingly small compared to what you imagine it could become. And then you begin.

The beginning is always the hardest part. Not because it requires the most skill, but because it requires the most courage. To start is to admit that you care. And caring, in a world that rewards indifference, is its own kind of bravery.

So build the thing. Write the words. Start the studio. Launch the page. It will not be perfect. It was never supposed to be.

title: Fragments
excerpt: I collect moments the way some people collect receipts — proof that something happened, that I was there.
content:
I collect moments
the way some people collect receipts —
proof that something happened,
that I was there.

A coffee gone cold.
The particular blue of 5am.
Your laugh caught mid-sentence,
sharp and unguarded.

I keep them in no particular order.
They do not need to make sense.
They only need to be mine.
